description: Dusk may end a child's play, and 'tei norawhi' says that the play lasts to that point. 'tei' marks the endpoint reached by an ongoing event or extent. 'phoe' (ANT) merely places one event before another; it does not say that the earlier event continues to that boundary.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper front teeth for unvoiced dental 't' and release it into middle-height front 'e'. The tongue then rises into high-front unrounded 'i' for the separate second syllable. Stress 'te'.
usage_notes: The endpoint follows 'tei'. It may be a time such as dusk or a named boundary such as 'lumae' (end).
